sg_weights._note: Shinnecock Hills: Par-70, ~7445y, links-style major. USGA setup — firm, fast, penal rough. APP is the primary differentiator (160-190y approach distances, severe penalty for missing greens). ARG elevated due to tight lies, runoffs, and wind. PUTT moderate (poa annua/fescue mix, variable speeds under wind). OTT neutral — accuracy trumps distance on narrow corridors. Knowledge-based estimate: no Shinnecock entry in COURSE_DNA_LIBRARY.

course_weight_override._note: Standard weights. Only 2018 Shinnecock history available (1 year under event_id=26). Expect LOW field coverage (<25%). Do NOT upgrade to 0.24/0.19 unless Phase 1 returns >40% coverage. For no/low-history major, ceiling weight should be 0.07 (vs 0.05 standard) per PGA Championship week 15 improvement suggestion — consider applying via MOMUS manual override or next Apollo run.

course_history_note: Shinnecock Hills last hosted U.S. Open in 2018 (Brooks Koepka win, +1). DataGolf event_id=26 confirmed to contain 2018 data (156 players, course_name='Shinnecock Hills GC'). Prior hosting was 2004 (Goosen) — may also be under event_id=26 but DataGolf SG data quality for 2004 is uncertain. Apollo Phase 1 should pull event_id=26 year=2018 only. If Apollo attempts multi-year range 2018-2025 and pulls non-Shinnecock venues (e.g. Oakmont 2025, Pinehurst 2024), add course_name filter or restrict year_range to [2018,2018] only. No fallback entry needed in COURSE_HISTORY_FALLBACKS.json for event_id=26 (standard direct lookup at correct year).
winner_archetype: Elite precision ball-striker with US Open-proven mental toughness. Shinnecock Hills rewards complete games — accurate driving into narrow corridors, elite iron play from 160-190y (the primary scoring distances), functional scrambling on tight fescue lies, and consistent putting on fast variable-speed poa/fescue greens. Koepka (2018) won with relentless par-saving and patient management. Typical US Open winner: ranked in top-30 globally, 4-6 previous US Open appearances, above-average make_cut probability, not necessarily the longest hitter but among the most accurate. Strong course-fit candidates: players with elite SG_APP, solid SG_ARG, and previous major-championship close finishes. Wind is a decisive wildcard — check ATHENA weather report before construction.
bbi_pipeline_action: FULL — Standard individual BBI v8.5 rankings. Major championship mode (is_major=true). Course history limited to 2018 Shinnecock only (event_id=26). Expect LOW field coverage (<25%). Ceiling weight elevation recommended (+0.02 vs standard, per PGA Championship week 15 suggestion). Weather variance likely — ATHENA should note wind conditions for DFS lineup construction. LIV players competing (major exemption) — verify field coverage includes Koepka, DeChambeau, Rahm, etc.
course_notes: Shinnecock Hills Golf Club, Southampton NY. Par-70, ~7445y. Links-style parkland with fescue rough, pot bunkers, and persistent Long Island Sound wind. USGA setup: firm+fast, severe rough penalty (1.5-4 inches progressive). One of the most demanding US Open venues. Notable: 2018 final round was controversially overcooked by USGA (course played to +20 in afternoon, USGA watered greens for final groups). Koepka won at +1, Fleetwood shot 63 in R4 (major record). LIV players may swell the field beyond usual 156 — verify with DG field pull.
pipeline_warning: ACTION REQUIRED: Apollo Phase 1 must filter course history to year=2018 ONLY for event_id=26. The US Open rotates venues — pulling 2019-2025 would include Pebble Beach, Winged Foot, Torrey Pines, Oakmont, Pinehurst, LACC, Pinehurst — none are Shinnecock. If Apollo uses a year_range without course_name filtering, all those years will contaminate course history with wrong-venue data (same error as RBC Canadian Open 2022-2024 contamination). Verify course_name='Shinnecock Hills' filter is applied before promoting to rankings.
